caption: Example of cooling the burn under running water
content_description: An arm burn being held under cool running water

caption: Applying pressure with a clean bandage
content_description: A clean bandage being pressed firmly on an arm wound

caption: Apply firm direct pressure to the wound
content_description: A responder pressing directly on a bleeding arm wound with gloved fingers.

caption: Leave the embedded object in place and press around it
content_description: A responder padding around an embedded object instead of removing it.

caption: Rinse the wound gently with clean water
content_description: A responder flushing a small open wound on the forearm with clean water.

caption: Pinch the soft part of the nose to slow the bleeding
content_description: A seated person pinching the soft part of the nose during a nosebleed.

caption: Help the person use their prescribed inhaler as directed
content_description: A child using a prescribed inhaler with the mouthpiece sealed at the lips.

caption: If prescribed, connect the inhaler to the spacer before use
content_description: Hands attaching an inhaler to a spacer device before assisting with medication use.

caption: Support the forearm with padding and an improvised rigid splint
content_description: A forearm secured against a padded improvised splint with cloth wraps.

caption: Immobilize the leg with padding and ties above and below the injury
content_description: A leg immobilized with padding and multiple cloth ties to keep the limb still.

caption: Secure a lower-leg splint with padding and ties
content_description: A lower leg supported with padding and tied in place to limit movement.

caption: Place the heel of one hand in the center of the chest
content_description: A rescuer placing both hands in the center of the chest for CPR compressions.

caption: Turn the AED on and follow the voice prompts
content_description: A gloved hand pressing the power button on an AED.

caption: Attach the AED pads to the exposed chest as shown
content_description: An AED pad being attached to an adult chest in the recommended position.

caption: For a child, attach the AED pads in the position shown on the pad diagrams
content_description: An AED pad being attached to a child chest using the placement shown on the pad.

caption: Give firm back blows between the shoulder blades
content_description: A hand positioned to deliver a back blow between the shoulder blades.

caption: Place the fist just above the navel for abdominal thrusts
content_description: A close-up showing fist placement just above the navel for an abdominal thrust.

caption: Stand behind the person and deliver abdominal thrusts inward and upward
content_description: A rescuer standing behind a choking adult to deliver abdominal thrusts.

caption: If the person becomes unresponsive, lower them to the floor and start chest compressions
content_description: A rescuer performing chest compressions on an unresponsive choking adult.

caption: Reach with a long object from shore instead of entering the water
content_description: A rescuer on shore using a long branch to reach a person in the water.

caption: Throw a flotation aid to the person if you cannot reach them
content_description: A rescuer throwing a flotation aid attached to a rope toward a person in the water.

caption: If available, row out in a boat instead of swimming directly to the person
content_description: A rescuer in a boat using an oar to reach a person in the water.

caption: Check for facial droop when the person smiles
content_description: A face showing one-sided drooping consistent with a FAST stroke check.

caption: Ask the person to raise both arms and look for weakness or drift
content_description: A person raising both arms unevenly during a FAST stroke check.

caption: Cool the person as fast as possible
content_description: A rescuer actively cooling a person with suspected heat stroke near the waterline.

caption: Loosely cover the burn with a clean dressing
content_description: A clean rectangular dressing being placed gently over a burn on the forearm.

caption: Use the person's prescribed epinephrine auto-injector
content_description: Two prescribed epinephrine auto-injector devices shown side by side.

caption: Place the auto-injector against the outer thigh
content_description: A responder positioning an epinephrine auto-injector against the outer thigh of a seated child.

caption: Press and hold the injector in the outer thigh as directed
content_description: A close-up of an epinephrine auto-injector pressed into the outer thigh during use.

caption: Wrap the person fully and keep cold air off the body
content_description: A person wrapped in blankets and reflective material during hypothermia care.

caption: Use a sleeping bag or thick wrap and insulate from the ground
content_description: A person enclosed in a sleeping bag setup with insulating material underneath.

caption: Wrap the person and warm the core first
content_description: A person wrapped in blankets holding a warm drink during rewarming for mild hypothermia.

caption: Open the airway and look for normal breathing
content_description: A responder opening the airway with head tilt and chin lift while checking breathing.

caption: Open the airway and look, listen, and feel for normal breathing
content_description: A responder tilting the head and listening close to the mouth while checking breathing.

caption: Apply gentle pressure to a scalp cut with a clean dressing
content_description: Gloved hands holding a clean pad against a bleeding scalp wound.

caption: Support the head in the position you found it
content_description: A rescuer stabilizing the head of a cyclist lying still after a crash.

caption: Keep the person still and support the head without forcing movement
content_description: A rescuer supporting the head of an injured cyclist while the helmet remains in place.

caption: Protect the head with something soft during the seizure
content_description: A jacket being positioned under the head of a person lying on the ground during a seizure.
